algorithms.title: Applying Anatomic/Developmental Context to Any Pediatric Encounter
algorithms.description: The foundational sequence for interpreting any pediatric finding in age- and development-appropriate context.
steps: Identify the child's age band before interpreting any vital sign or exam finding.
Recall the anatomic differences relevant to the presenting complaint (airway: narrow/anterior/subglottic; breathing: high demand/low reserve; circulation: small volume/strong compensation).
Compare vital signs to the age-appropriate normal range, not an adult or generic pediatric reference.
Adapt your communication and exam approach to the child's developmental stage to maximize cooperation and accuracy.
Weigh all findings together (overall gestalt) rather than relying on any single number, especially blood pressure, which is a late sign in pediatric shock.
